scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within biostatistics and data visualization scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies detected; outputs apply standard approaches appropriate to biostatistics and data visualization tasks.

code_usability.detail: Generated code executed without syntax errors or unresolvable dependency failures across all tested inputs.

functional_suitability.note: Time-varying covariate now explicitly out-of-scope with R redirect; KM, log-rank, Cox all covered

reliability.note: Fallback template present; Statistical Caveats section now mandatory in Output Requirements

performance_context.note: No references/ folder; single SKILL.md at 153 lines — acceptable but no progressive disclosure

agent_usability.note: Workflow step 3 now emits explicit note when --group absent; Statistical Caveats section added to Response Template

human_usability.note: Description is discoverable; trigger language natural for clinical researchers

security.note: No credential handling needed; local file I/O only; no input sanitization for CSV column names

maintainability.note: Clean structure; references/ folder present; scripts separated; all P1/P2 fixes applied cleanly

agent_specific.note: Time-varying covariate now in out-of-scope list; escape hatches strengthened; composability still limited by file-path dependency

key_strengths: Comprehensive statistical methods section with formulas (KM estimator, Greenwood variance, Cox PH) demonstrates methodological rigor
Time-varying covariate now cleanly refused with R survival package redirect, closing the primary scope gap from v1
Mandatory Statistical Caveats section in Output Requirements ensures high-risk scenario warnings are consistently surfaced
Fallback template with exact lifelines install command reduces friction on environment failures
Clean separation of output files (curve PNG, stats CSV, HR CSV, log-rank CSV, report TXT) supports reproducible research workflows

recommendations.title: Refusal message wording may deviate from template
recommendations.problem: The scope refusal for time-varying covariate requests occasionally includes brief methodological context beyond the documented template, creating minor inconsistency.
recommendations.root_cause: Input Validation section specifies the refusal message but does not mark it as verbatim-required.
recommendations.fix: Add 'respond verbatim:' before the refusal message template in Input Validation, matching the pattern used in variant-pathogenicity-predictor.

recommendations.title: No references/ folder for progressive disclosure
recommendations.problem: All content is in a single SKILL.md at 153 lines. A references/ folder for statistical methods details would improve token efficiency for agents that do not need the full formula section.
recommendations.root_cause: Skill was designed as a self-contained document without progressive disclosure.
recommendations.fix: Consider moving the Statistical Methods section to references/statistical_methods.md and referencing it from SKILL.md to reduce context load for simple requests.
